title: scrophularia alata proles cinerea
intro: Scrophularia alata proles cinerea, also known as the Ashy-flowered Figwort, is a plant species belonging to the Figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its content of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Scrophularia alata proles cinerea include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
